input:
I have a question about proration math on invoice TL-2026-59124 — no complaint about the legitimacy of the charges, just the calculation.

We are on the Business plan, annual billing (20% discount), renewal date January 3. On May 14 I added two seats mid-cycle. The invoice shows Seat A prorated at $61.27 and Seat B prorated at $68.46 — added the same day, same plan, yet different amounts. Working backwards, Seat A appears to include the 20% annual discount on the prorated period and Seat B appears not to.

The difference is only $7.19, and I recognize the amount is immaterial — but our auditors sample these line items, and I need to either explain the calculation or get it corrected. Could you share the exact proration formula (days remaining or months remaining; discount applied before or after proration?) and confirm whether Seat B was computed correctly? If it's a rounding rule, just point me at the documentation. No rush over the weekend.
expected: {"category": "billing", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Customer questions inconsistent annual discount on mid-cycle seat proration; requests calculation documentation."}
predicted: {"category": "how_to", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Proration formula applies annual discount before proration; Seat B missing discount, calculation error."}

input:
Long-time listener, first-time... no wait, fourteenth-time caller. Here's this month's bug.

Drag-and-drop task reordering on the web app no longer saves. I drag my five tasks into priority order — like deck chairs, if you will — everything looks right, and then I refresh and they snap back to their previous order. Every time.

Because I've done this support dance before, here's the part you'd normally have to ask me for: Chrome 126 and Firefox latest, both do it. Pro plan. Started roughly two weeks ago. No console errors that I can see, though I admit I only opened dev tools to feel powerful. Crucially: if I reorder on the iOS app instead, the order DOES stick, and even shows correctly on web afterwards. So mobile writes, web doesn't.

It's not killing me — I've adapted by doing all my reordering on a phone like an animal — but it is definitely broken. Your move.
expected: {"category": "bug", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Web task reordering reverts after refresh; mobile app saves order correctly."}
predicted: {"category": "bug", "urgency": "high", "summary": "Web drag-and-drop task reordering fails to save; iOS works, web does not"}

input:
Dear Taskloop,

I am 74 and mostly retired and I use your free version to organize three things: my volunteer shifts, my medications, and our family reunion. Yesterday I tried to start a fourth board for my quilting circle and a message said I have reached my limit of 3 boards.

My neighbor said I should use a 'template' instead but when I clicked templates it wanted to make ANOTHER new board, which it won't let me do. I confess I do not understand the difference between a board and a template, and I don't want to accidentally erase my medication list experimenting.

Is there a way to put the quilting circle inside one of my other boards? Or do I have to pay the $9.99? I am not against paying but I want to understand what I am buying first.

Sincerely,
Eleanor

input:
I received an email this morning purporting to be from Taskloop Billing, stating our payment failed and instructing me to 'verify card details within 24 hours' via a link to taskloop-billing-secure dot com. The sender address does not match your domain, and the invoice number referenced does not exist in our records — I checked it against all twelve invoices we have on file.

I have not clicked the link and have reported it internally. input:
This is the second notice regarding invoice irregularities on our Business account. On the 1st of this month we were charged twice for the same billing period — two identical charges of $1,098.90, same day, same card, and the billing page itself shows two invoices with identical line items and period dates. This is unambiguous.

I reported this through the in-app form five days ago and have received nothing beyond the auto-acknowledgement. Our finance department has now escalated internally. Per company policy on unresolved duplicate vendor charges, they will initiate a chargeback with our bank this Friday if the duplicate is not refunded before then.

I would genuinely prefer to resolve this directly — a chargeback creates paperwork for everyone and I have no other complaints about the service. But Friday is a hard deadline that I do not control. Please have someone from billing reply with a refund confirmation and timeline before Thursday close of business. Reference: both invoice numbers are visible on our account billing page this month.
expected: {"category": "billing", "urgency": "critical", "summary": "Duplicate charge unresolved five days; finance initiating chargeback Friday"}
predicted: {"category": "billing", "urgency": "high", "summary": "Duplicate charges of $1,098.90 detected; refund required within 48 hours or chargeback initiated."}

input:
Found what looks like a pagination bug in the REST API and it's quietly corrupting our compliance exports.

Context: Business plan, we export all tasks across all boards weekly for an audit trail (regulated industry). Script walks GET /boards/{id}/tasks with per_page=100 using the cursor from meta.next_cursor.

The bug: on boards with more than ~700 tasks, some cursors return a page we've already received — identical task IDs, same 100 records — while meta.next_cursor keeps advancing. Net effect: duplicates in our dataset AND, worse, some tasks never appear at all. I diffed a paginated export against per-task GETs for one board: one 100-task page duplicated, 63 tasks missing entirely. Missing records in an audit dataset is a serious problem for us.

Repro details: happens consistently on our two biggest boards (~800 and ~1,150 tasks), never on boards under ~700. Ordering param default. I can provide board IDs, full request/response logs with cursors, and the diff — just tell me where to send them, ideally to an engineer rather than a first-line queue.

For now I've switched to fetching per-task as a workaround, which is slow and rate-limit hungry but correct. Would appreciate confirmation this is reproducible on your side and a fix timeline, since our auditors ask about data completeness, and 'our PM tool's API loses records' is not an answer I want to give them.
expected: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "API pagination returns duplicate pages and omits tasks on large boards."}
predicted: {"category": "bug", "urgency": "high", "summary": "Pagination bug causes task duplicates and missing records in large boards; affects compliance exports."}

input:
I just got the renewal notice and I need to say this somewhere official. When I signed up two years ago this was a reasonable tool at a reasonable price. Now with my team at 11 seats I'm staring at over $1,300 a year and honestly the product hasn't gotten $1,300 better.

I've already moved our client work into a spreadsheet to test if we'd survive without you, and the ugly truth is we mostly would. So before that renewal charges on the 30th, downgrade my account to the Free plan. I understand we'll only keep 3 boards — tell me what happens to the other boards' data, because if you delete it the second I downgrade, that's the kind of thing that turns a downgrade into a one-star review.

I'll keep one seat eyes-open in case the pricing comes back to earth. Confirm the downgrade is processed before the 30th.

predicted: {"category": "billing", "urgency": "high", "summary": "Request to downgrade from 11 seats to Free plan prior to renewal date; confirm data retention for non-kept boards."}
